comment_text.sn12.39:1.4: “Conceived” (avakkanti) is one of the synonyms of “rebirth” (SN 12.2:4.2). Without conception, name and form would not take shape (literally “coagulate”) in the womb (DN 15:21.2).

root_text.sn12.39:1.2: “Yañca, bhikkhave, ceteti yañca pakappeti yañca anuseti, ārammaṇametaṁ hoti viññāṇassa ṭhitiyā.
root_text.sn12.39:1.4: Tasmiṁ patiṭṭhite viññāṇe virūḷhe nāmarūpassa avakkanti hoti.
root_text.sn12.39:2.1: No ce, bhikkhave, ceteti no ce pakappeti, atha ce anuseti, ārammaṇametaṁ hoti viññāṇassa ṭhitiyā.
root_text.sn12.39:2.3: Tasmiṁ patiṭṭhite viññāṇe virūḷhe nāmarūpassa avakkanti hoti.
root_text.sn12.39:3.1: Yato ca kho, bhikkhave, no ceva ceteti no ca pakappeti no ca anuseti, ārammaṇametaṁ na hoti viññāṇassa ṭhitiyā.
root_text.sn12.39:3.2: Ārammaṇe asati patiṭṭhā viññāṇassa na hoti.
root_text.sn12.39:3.3: Tadappatiṭṭhite viññāṇe avirūḷhe nāmarūpassa avakkanti na hoti.

translation_text.sn12.39:1.2: “Mendicants, what you intend or plan, and what you have underlying tendencies for become a support for the continuation of consciousness.
translation_text.sn12.39:1.3: When this support exists, consciousness becomes established.
translation_text.sn12.39:1.4: When consciousness is established, name and form are conceived.
translation_text.sn12.39:1.5: Name and form are requirements for the six sense fields.
translation_text.sn12.39:1.6: The six sense fields are requirements for contact.
translation_text.sn12.39:1.7: Contact is a requirement for feeling. …
translation_text.sn12.39:1.13: sorrow, lamentation, pain, sadness, and distress come to be.
translation_text.sn12.39:1.14: That is how this entire mass of suffering originates.
translation_text.sn12.39:2.1: If you don’t intend or plan, but still have underlying tendencies, this becomes a support for the continuation of consciousness.
translation_text.sn12.39:2.2: When this support exists, consciousness becomes established.
translation_text.sn12.39:2.3: When consciousness is established, name and form are conceived.
translation_text.sn12.39:2.4: Name and form are requirements for the six sense fields. …
translation_text.sn12.39:2.5: That is how this entire mass of suffering originates.
translation_text.sn12.39:3.1: If you don’t intend or plan or have underlying tendencies, this doesn’t become a support for the continuation of consciousness.
translation_text.sn12.39:3.2: With no support, consciousness is not established.
translation_text.sn12.39:3.3: When consciousness is not established and doesn’t grow, name and form are not conceived.
translation_text.sn12.39:3.4: When name and form cease, the six sense fields cease. …
translation_text.sn12.39:3.5: That is how this entire mass of suffering ceases.”
